sources_exist.note: All seven canonical citation occurrences resolve at their exact URLs, including both PDFs.

figures_match_sources.note: Only the IAB citation cleanly supports its quoted aggregate 30% full-scale adoption figure. The other six citations do not support their complete score-affecting claims: they do not establish three named active 541830 consolidators, the selected 4.5x and 8.5x scale-specific media-buying multiples, a 4-5-year adoption analog, 35% ownership age 60+, or the asserted independent-agency pricing mix.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The six role shares are mutually exclusive and sum to 1.0, and their products sum exactly to ai_replaceable_share=0.514. However, owners_60plus_pct=0.35 is not derivable from 37% aged 55-73, active_consolidators=3 is admitted to be an estimate without an enumerated 541830 platform set, and pi_dist=0.55 relies on a pricing interpretation contradicted by the cited survey's fee-majority result. The selected buy and exit multiples also mix broad digital/marketing agency scopes rather than comparable media-buying transactions.

rubric_consistent.note: The deterministic inputs and formulas reproduce V=5.672504, C=1.925, A=3.3333333333, B=5.5934356909, M=3.5555555556, and geometric-mean S=3.7319350918. The Stage-4 ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL flag is valid: stored B differs by about 0.00000769 and stored S by about 0.00006491, both immaterial display-level deltas. Correct arithmetic does not cure the unsupported B, M, A, and capture inputs required by the frozen rubric.

cross_checks_honest.note: All mandatory cross-checks are present and the PRESSURED classification is directionally plausible, but pricing_model_deflation misstates the 2019 ANA survey as a 2024 transaction mix, converts service-specific respondent method rates into a 40/35/15 billing mix that sums to only 90%, and treats commission billing as dominant although the survey says most advertisers use fees. The t50 narrative also says the cited trajectory would cross 50% in 2025-2026, then selects mid-2029 without evidence for that three-year delay.

source_audits.note: The FE International article describes broad agency and marketing-services consolidation and the generic platform-and-bolt-on playbook. It does not name Tinuiti, Wpromote, Power Digital, Bounteous, or New Engen, does not enumerate three active platforms specific to NAICS 541830, and reports a 13% rather than 14% increase for adtech, martech, and digital-content M&A.

Axial reports rough broad-agency ranges of 3.5-5x for $1M-plus EBITDA and 4-7x for $2M-plus EBITDA, while its observed smaller-agency average is 3.33x around $500K EBITDA. It does not report a 4.5x average for $1-3M EBITDA media-buying agencies or validate the record's mapping from the 10-99 employee band to that multiple.

The IAB report's total sample of 529 agencies, brands, and publishers shows 30% at full-scale deployment across the media campaign lifecycle, matching the quoted aggregate figure. Applicability is weaker than the citation wording suggests because the agency subgroup is 37%, not 30%, and the sample is not restricted to NAICS 541830.

First Page Sage reports $5-10M EBITDA averages by broad agency type, including 9.0x for digital marketing, 9.3x for performance marketing, and 9.5x for advertising. It neither reports 8.5x for performance-media platforms nor provides a $10M-plus EBITDA media-services category or the record's asserted AI-disintermediation discount.

The MarTech article supports a 2014 forecast of $10B and 45% of U.S. digital display spend, plus a forecast above $20B by 2016. It does not report 61% or more in 2018, a 2011-2012 baseline adoption percentage, or an observed 2015-2016 crossing, so it cannot establish the quoted 4-5-year time-to-50% analog.

Oberlo reports that 37% of general small-business owners are ages 55-73. It provides no age-60-plus split, no NAICS 541830 data, and none of the quoted 13.0%-to-16.3% age-65-plus self-employment series; 35% age 60+ cannot be inferred from this page.

The September 2019 ANA survey does report commission use by 40% of respondents for programmatic services, 24% for digital/search/social buying, and 12% for traditional media buying. The record misdates this as a 2024 benchmark, changes traditional buying to general agency services, and claims commission dominance among independents even though the report says the majority use labor-based or fixed fees; it does not support the asserted overall pricing mix.

reasons: At inputs.active_consolidators (https://www.feinternational.com/blog/agency-marketing-ma-consolidation-ai-exit-opportunities), a broad discussion of agency roll-ups does not establish value=3 active consolidators for NAICS 541830 or evidence the platforms named in the record. Re-run with an enumerated platform list and dated media-buying-agency add-ons for every counted consolidator.

At inputs.buy_mult (https://www.axial.net/forum/digital-marketing-agency-valuation-multiples/), broad digital-agency ranges do not establish a 4.5x observed acquisition multiple for 10-99-employee media-buying firms. Re-run with comparable transactions at the target scale and document the EBITDA and owner-comp normalization.

At inputs.exit_mult (https://firstpagesage.com/business/marketing-agency-valuation-multiples-and-valuations/), the source's $5-10M broad-agency table reports 9.0x-9.5x for the nearest categories and does not support the selected 8.5x, a $10M-plus platform scope, or an AI-risk adjustment. Re-run with recent platform-scale media-buying transactions or credible disclosed comparables.

At inputs.historical_analogs (https://martech.org/us-programmatic-ad-spend-10-billion-2014-double-2016-emarketer/), the cited page supplies only the 2014 45% forecast and 2016 dollar forecast, not the claimed 2011-2012 baseline, 2018 penetration, or measured crossing. Re-run t50_years with a single coherent adoption series and a documented start and 50% crossing.

At inputs.owners_60plus_pct (https://www.oberlo.com/statistics/average-age-of-small-business-owners), 37% aged 55-73 cannot support value=0.35 aged 60+ and the page contains no 541830 adjustment. Re-run with age bins that permit a transparent 60+ calculation and industry-specific evidence where available.

At inputs.pricing_structure and inputs.pi_dist (https://s3.amazonaws.com/media.mediapost.com/uploads/MediaAgencyCompensation.pdf), the record turns a 2019 advertiser survey's service-specific method rates into an unsupported claim that commissions dominate independents and mislabels the result as 2024. Re-run with a current, scope-matched billing survey and derive pi_dist from actual revenue or contract shares.

At inputs.t50_years, the narrative says the IAB trajectory implies crossing 50% in 2025-2026, but value=3.0 places the crossing around mid-2029. Re-run with an explicit lag model for small media-buying firms or select a time consistent with the cited evidence.

At cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation, the asserted 40% commission, 35% fixed-fee, and 15% principal mix totals only 90% and is not what the ANA survey measures; the document instead reports method choices by media service and a fee majority. Re-run the cross-check using exhaustive, mutually exclusive billing shares and clearly separate operator labor savings from client renewal-price pressure.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, S
